codex_build_instructions.instruction_to_codex: Build the MVP as a working local Streamlit application using this XML/Markdown file series as the source of truth. Prioritize a runnable Phase 1 app before adding API ingestion.

build_order.step: Create repository tree exactly as described in technical_architecture.xml.
Create requirements.txt with streamlit, duckdb, pandas, plotly, pyyaml, python-dotenv, requests, pyarrow.
Create scripts/build_db.py to load sample CSVs to DuckDB.
Add visible score explanations and CSV downloads.
Add placeholder ingestion scripts that are safe and do not require keys for sample mode.

codex_build_instructions.initial_commands: python -m venv .venv .venv\Scripts\activate # Windows PowerShell: .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 pip install -r requirements.txt python scripts/build_db.py streamlit run app.py

critical_constraints.constraint: Do not put API keys in code, tests, sample files, screenshots, or README examples.
Do not call USAspending or SAM APIs from Streamlit page load.
Sample data must be easy to delete and replace.
Use clear comments where Phase 2 API logic will be expanded.
Favor simple, readable code over excessive abstraction.

sample_records_requirement.record: Create at least 8 agencies, 12 vendors, 20 awards, 12 opportunities, 6 vehicles, and 6 capture notes.
Include Navy, NAVSEA, Team Submarine, CDAO, DISA, Air Force, Army, and one civilian agency.
Use fictionalized or clearly sample records where needed.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Codex produces a runnable local app in one pass or with minimal follow-up.
README accurately describes local setup and future API ingestion.
